strengths: Successfully completed all phases of the task including final suture cut
Maintained good needle control throughout the procedure
Demonstrated consistent knot tying technique across all three throws
weaknesses: Suture placement was off target by approximately 2mm from one marked site
Missed a required hand exchange between the first and second throws
Completion time of 234 seconds indicates room for efficiency improvement

drills.name: Mark-to-mark needle path drill
drills.description: Practice 15 precise needle passes through both target marks, pausing to verify exact placement before proceeding to knot tying.
drills.targets_weakness: Suture placement accuracy at target marks.

drills.name: Alternating throw ladder
drills.description: Perform 10 three-throw sequences with deliberate hand exchange between each throw, emphasizing the transition between first and second throws.

drills.name: Economy-of-motion ladder
drills.description: Complete the full task sequence slowly with minimal instrument repositioning and efficient movement patterns to reduce overall time.

progression_note: At the advanced level, focus on precision refinements and approaching the published benchmark with minimal deductions. Your technique is solid but requires attention to exact target placement and consistent hand exchange protocols.
